text: Since the early days of socialism, propaganda has often been the first word on the lips of those who talk about communication in Eastern Europe. The USSR was the first propaganda state in world history, a term coined by Peter Kenez in his 1985 work on Soviet society of the revolutionary period. Kenez describes the Bolshevik regime as the first to achieve real propaganda objectives by way of political education, with the aim of creating a new type of man appropriate to the new society. There was “a conscious effort to create a new type of man for the new society. Propaganda was used to spread revolutionary ideology and to raise the political level of the toiling masses, and thereby to help to create the kind of citizen that would be appropriate to a rational society based on socialism”. The idea of creating a “new man” was central to the attempts to restructure society carried out by the revolutionary regime.
headline: Fully Human Written
